title: Role of Oxidant Stress and Gender in Erythrocytes Arginine Metabolism in Type 2 Diabetes
author: Martha L. Contreras-Zentella
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.q5qdy5w

steps.title: a) Whole blood was poured directly...
steps.instruction: a) Whole blood was poured directly into ice-cold perchloric acid (8% w/v, final concentration). After centrifugation, we obtained perchloric acid-extracts of serum and RBC. b) Whole blood was centrifugated to separate RBC and serum samples 8 We extract proteins from blood Perchloric acid Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #244252 Be carefull, avoind contact with skin, eyes and do not inhale Undetermined, on ice

steps.title: The perchloric acid-extracts obtained in the...
steps.instruction: The perchloric acid-extracts obtained in the previous step were neutralized: we added sodium hydroxide until we reach a pH of 7. These preparations were used to detemine the following metabolites: ammonia, urea, arginine, citrulline, ornithine, nitrites, malondialdehyde (MDA), uric acid. Sodium hydroxide Bio Basic Inc. Catalog #SB0617.SIZE.500G

steps.title: For the determination of glucose, insulin,...
steps.instruction: For the determination of glucose, insulin, HbA1C, triglycerides, cholesterol, alanina aminotransferasa (ALT), and aspartato aminotransferasa (AST), we only used serum samples (1b).

steps.title: Glucose was determined in serum samples...
steps.instruction: Glucose was determined in serum samples with the glucose oxidase reaction using the Glucosa-LQ GOD-POD kit (SPINREACT). The color developed was read at 450 nm using Biotek instruments. (Biotek instruments, ELx800, USA)

steps.title: Insulin was determined in serum samples...
steps.instruction: Insulin was determined in serum samples with the Human Insulin ELISA kit (ELH-Insulin-2 RayBio, USA). The color developed was read at 450 nm using Biotek instruments. (Biotek instruments, EL x 800, USA)

steps.title: HbA1C was determined in serum samples...
steps.instruction: HbA1C was determined in serum samples with the glycosylated HbA1c kit (BoiSys-Kovalent, Brazil; 4190045K R1 2). The color developed was read at 450 nm using Biotek instruments. (Biotek instruments, EL x 800, USA)

steps.title: Triglycerides were determined in serum samples...
steps.instruction: Triglycerides were determined in serum samples with the GPO-POD enzimatic colorimetric kit (Spinreact, Spain). The color developed was read at 450 nm using Biotek instruments. (Biotek instruments, EL x 800, USA)

steps.title: Uric acid was determined in serum...
steps.instruction: Uric acid was determined in serum samples with the Uricase-POD Enzimatic colorimetric kit (Spinreact, Spain). The color developed was read at 450 nm using Biotek instruments. (Biotek instruments, EL x 800, USA)

steps.title: AST was determined in serum samples with the NADH
steps.instruction: AST was determined in serum samples with the NADH.Cinetic UV.IFCC kit (Spinreact, Spain). The color developed was read at 450 nm using Biotek instruments. (Biotek instruments, EL x 800, USA)

steps.title: ALT was determined in serum samples with the NADH
steps.instruction: ALT was determined in serum samples with the NADH.Cinetic UV.IFCC kit (Spinreact, Spain). The color developed was read at 450 nm using Biotek instruments. (Biotek instruments, EL x 800, USA)

steps.title: Cholesterol was determined in serum samples...
steps.instruction: Cholesterol was determined in serum samples with the CHOD-POD lit (Spinreact,Spain). The color developed was read at 450 nm using Biotek instruments. (Biotek instruments, EL x 800, USA)

steps.title: MDA was quantified by the thiobarbituric...
steps.instruction: MDA was quantified by the thiobarbituric acid method using acid extracts from RBC and serum (6% perchloric acid w/v). Samples of these extracts (0.5ml) were mixed with 1.5 ml of 20% acetic acid (adjusted to pH 2.5 with KOH) and with 1.5 ml of 8% thiobarbituric acid. 8 , 8% 2-thiobarbituric acid Potassium hydroxide Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P1767 2-Thiobarbituric acid Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Perchloric acid Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #244252 Acetic Acid, Glacial Meyer Catalog #0040 Potassium hydroxide Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P1767 2-Thiobarbituric acid Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) 6 , 6% perchloric acid w/v 20 , 20% acetic acid

steps.title: The samples were kept for 45...
steps.instruction: The samples were kept for 45 minutes a boiling water bath and 1 ml of 2% KCL was added at the end of the incubation to each sample. 00:45:00, boiling water bath 2 , 2% KCL undetermined, of 2% KCL

steps.title: The color complex formed at the...
steps.instruction: The color complex formed at the end of the incubation (in the last step) was extracted with the mix butanol-pyridin (15:1, v/v) and detected at 532 nm in a spectrophotometer (Beckman Coulter). The extinction coefficient of malonaldehyde color complex was 1.56 x 105 cm -1 M -1 , as determined using a standard curve with tetraethoxypropane. 15 , mix butanol-pyridin (15:1, v/v) (Beckman Coulter, DU 640, Spectrophotometer) 1,1,3,3-Tetraethoxypropane Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #9889

steps.title: Content of arginine was determined in...
steps.instruction: Content of arginine was determined in neutralized perchloric extracts of RBC and serum as arginine converts to ornithine using rat liver cytosol-containing activated arginase. Liver cytosol enzyme was activated by a 20 min-incubation with 10 mM Manganese chloride (MnCl2) at 55 ºC. 00:20:00, 20 minutes incubation Undetermined, for incubation 10 , 10mM Manganese chloride (MnCl2) Manganese (II) chloride tetrahydrate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #221279

steps.title: To a sample of 0
steps.instruction: To a sample of 0.5 ml acid-extract, 0.3 ml of sodium carbonates buffer (pH = 9.5) was added and the reaction started with 10 µg of cytosolic protein. The mixture was incubated by 40 min at 37 ºC, and stopped by addition of 1.5 ml of acetic acid. Undetermined, incubation 00:40:00, incubation Sodium carbonate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #222321 undetermined, 10 µg of cytosolic protein. Acetic Acid, Glacial Meyer Catalog #0040

steps.title: The color resulting from the reaction...
steps.instruction: The color resulting from the reaction was developed through a ninhydrin solution (140 mM dissolved in acetic acid:6 M phosphoric acid, 6:4 v/v) in boiling water during 45 min. The colored was detected at 515 nm, contrasting with a standard curve for ornithine (30, 50, 100, 200 500, 1000 nmoles in the reaction mixture). Ninhydrin Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #N4876 Acetic Acid, Glacial Meyer Catalog #0040 Phosphoric acid Meyer Catalog #7664-38-2 140 , 140 mM dissolved in acetic acid:6 M phosphoric acid, 6:4 v/v 00:45:00

steps.title: Arginase was determined in RBC
steps.instruction: Arginase was determined in RBC. Five mg of hemoglobin from RBC lysate were activated for arginase activity by a 20 min-incubation with 10 mM MnCl 2. 00:20:00, incubation time Manganese (II) chloride tetrahydrate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #221279 undetermined, hemoglobin from RBC

steps.title: Samples were now incubated in 0
steps.instruction: Samples were now incubated in 0.3 ml sodium carbonate buffer (pH = 9.5) by 10 min at 37 ºC, and stopped by addition of 1.5 ml of acetic acid. 00:10:00, incubation Sodium carbonate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #223484 Undetermined, incubation temperature Acetic Acid, Glacial Meyer Catalog #0040

steps.title: The color was developed through a...
steps.instruction: The color was developed through a ninhydrin solution (140 mM dissolved in acetic acid:6 M phosphoric acid, 6:4 v/v) in boiling water by 45 min. The colored was detected at 515 nm in a spectrophotometer (Beckman Coulter), contrasting with a standard curve for ornithine (30,50, 100, 200 500, 1000 nmoles in the reaction mixture). Activity expressed as nmoles·min-1 ·mg of hemoglobin protein. Ninhydrin Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #N4876 Acetic Acid, Glacial Meyer Catalog #0040 Phosphoric acid Meyer Catalog #7664-38-2 00:45:00, duration in boiling water 140 , mM ninhydrin solution dissolved in acetic acid:6 M phosphoric acid, 6:4 v/v 6 , v/v acetic acid:6 M phosphoric acid (Beckman Coulter, DU 640, spectrophotometer)

steps.title: Add 1ml of any type of...
steps.instruction: Add 1ml of any type of sample (an extract, RBC or serum) 2 ml Drabkin solution. Drabkin´s solution: Dissolve 200 mg K3[(CN)6], 50 mg KCN, 140 mg KH2PO4. in 1000 ml water. We omitted the addition of antifoam because it is not avaiblable (no supply). The sample was centrifugated when needed. In this case we measured the absorbance of the supernatant at 546 nm. When centrifugation was not needed, the sample was directly measured in the spectrophotometer (Beckman Coulter). Calculate the concentration of hemoglobin (gr/l) with a hemoglobin cyanide-standard or using the molar absortion coefficient E=44 lnmol -1 /mm -1 . Molecular weight of Hb is 64 458. Potassium ferricyanide (III) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #702587 Potassium cyanide Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #207810 Monobasic potassium phosphate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #PHR1330 undetermined, K3[(CN)6] undetermined, KCN undetermined, KH2PO4 undetermined, water undetermined, Drabkin solution (Beckman Coulter, DU 640, Spectrophptometer)

steps.title: We prepared the following reagents
steps.instruction: We prepared the following reagents: Reagent A, 2 per cent NaCO3 in 0.10 N NaOH Reagent B, 0.5 per cent CuS04.5 H20 in 1 per cent potassium sodium tartrate. Reagent C, alkaline copper solution. Mix 50 ml. of Reagent A with 1 ml. of Reagent B. Discard after 1 day. Reagent D, carbonate-copper solution, is the same as Reagent C except for omission of NaOH. Reagent E, diluted Folin reagent (Sigma-Aldrich) diluted 1:1 in water. We prepared a solution from bovine serum albumine and used it as a standard curve. Sodium carbonate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #223484 Copper (II) sulfate pentahydrate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #209198 Potassium sodium tartrate tetrahydrate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #217255 Folin & Ciocalteus phenol Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #F9253 Bovine Serum Albumin (BSA) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #A7906

steps.title: Procedure for Proteins in Solution or...
steps.instruction: Procedure for Proteins in Solution or Readily Soluble in Dilute Alkali- (Directions are given for a final volume of 1.1 to 1.3 ml., but any multiple or fraction of the volumes given may be employed as desired’.) To a sample of 5 to 100 ul of protein in 0.2 ml. or less in a 3 to 10 ml. test-tube, add 1 ml. of Reagent C. Mix well and allow to stand for 10 minutes or longer at room temperature. 00:10:00

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.10 ml. of Reagent E very rapidly and mixed within a second or two (see below). After 30 minutes or longer, the sample is read in a calorimeter or spectrophotometer. For the range 5 to 25 ul of protein per ml. of final volume, it is desirable to make readings at or near 750 nm, the absorption peak. Calculate from a standard curve. 00:30:00 (Beckman, DU 640, Spectrophotometer)

steps.title: Ammonia reacts with 2-oxoglutarate and NADH...
steps.instruction: Ammonia reacts with 2-oxoglutarate and NADH to form l-glutamate in the presence of de glutamate dehydrogenase. As a result we obtain oxidized NADH (NAD). The amount of NADH consumed in the reaction is stoichiometric with the amount of ammonia. We use neutralized perchloric extracts of RBC or serum samples. All operations are made at 0-4°C. Pipette successively into the cuvettes: -1 ml of reaction buffer (2-oxoglutarate 35 mM; ADP: adenine diphosphate 1.82 mM; TEA: trietanolamine 0.5M. pH 8). - 0.10 ml NADH (6 mM) -0.10 - 0.5 ml sample Final volume with water to 3.2 ml. Mix and monitor change in absorbance until constant. Read absorbance (0). Undetermined, at all time before incubation Adenosine 5-diphosphate sodium salt Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #A2754 alpha-Ketoglutaric acid sodium salt Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #K1875 Beta-nicotinamide adenine dinuclotide, reduced disodium salt hydrate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #N8129 Triethanolamine Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #90279

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.02 ml GlDH (glutamate dehydrogenase,3,000 U/ml in triethanolamine 150 mM pH 8 buffer; diluted 1:400) Mix. After, read every 20 min until stability using a spectrophotometer. Calculate from a standard curve with NH4Cl solution. Glutamate dehydrogenase (Gldh) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #10197734001 Triethanolamine Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #90279 (Beckman Coulter, DU 640, spectrophotometer)

steps.title: Urea in the presence of urease...
steps.instruction: Urea in the presence of urease is transformed to 2 ammonium molecules, which is determined by the aforementioned method. Pipette successively into the cuvettes: -1.75 ml of reaction buffer ( Tris 150 mM, succinate 60 mM, 2-oxoglutarate 12.5 mM, ADP. Adenosine 5-diphosphate sodium salt 2 mM, pH 8) -0.1 ml NADH 5 mM - 0.66 ml GlDH (glutamate dehydrogenase,3,000 U/ml Tris_HCl buffer 150 mM pH 8; diluted 1:400) - 0.05 ml neutralized perchloric extracts of RBC or serum diluted 10-fold Mix thoroughly alter 10 min read (339 nm) A(0) 00:10:00, mix thoroughly Tris base Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #T1503 Succinic acid Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #398055 alpha-Ketoglutaric acid sodium salt Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #K1875 Adenosine 5-diphosphate sodium salt Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #A2754 Glutamate dehydrogenase (Gldh) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #10197734001

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.005 ml urease solution (2.5kU/L). Mix. After 15 min read ( 339 nm) the final absorbance A using a spectrophotometer. Used A – A(0)= delta A for calculation. Calculation The value thus obtained has to be multiplied by the factor of sample pre dilution and using a standard curve with NH4Cl solution. 00:15:00, mix Urease from Canavalia ensiformis (Jack bean) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #U7752 Ammonium chloride Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #254134 (Beckman Coulter, DU 640, spectrophotometer)

steps.title: We added water to 0
steps.instruction: We added water to 0.3 0.5 ml acid-extracts of serum or RBC to have a final volume of 1 ml. Mix. Add 0.5 ml acetic acid, glacial. Mix. Add 0.5 ml ninhydrin *. Mix. *ninhydrin solution: 250 mg ninhydrin in 6 ml glacial acetic, 4ml phosphoric acid 0.6 M. Put in boiling water bath for 10 min. Allow cooling to room temperature and add 3 ml EtOH 95% Read at 515 nm using a spectrophotometer. Calculation using a standard curve with 5, to 100 nmoles of ornithine. Acetic Acid, Glacial Meyer Catalog #0040 Ninhydrin Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #N4876 Phosphoric acid Meyer Catalog #7664-38-2 Ethanol absolute JT Baker Catalog #13225483 00:10:00, boiling water bath L-Ornithine monohydrochloride Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #O2375 (Beckman Coulter, DU640, spectrophotometer)

steps.title: The citrulline is formed from serum...
steps.instruction: The citrulline is formed from serum ornithine and carbamyl phosphate by ornithine carbamoyl transferase. This method is based on the citrulline determination by the diacetylmonoxime-phenazone reaction. For the colorimetric reaction, we add 0.2 - 0.5 ml of acid extracts of serum and RBC to a mixture* of 1.5 ml of diacetylmonoxime and phenazone, prepared immediately before use. *Phenazone: 4 g are dissolved in 1 L of 40% v/v sulfuric acid solution ; 50 mg of ferric sulfate are added. Diacetylmonoxime solution: 0.5% in 5% acetic acid. Sulfuric acid Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #258105 Iron (III) sulfate hydrate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #307718 2,3-Butanedione monoxine Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #31550 Phenazone Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P0800000 Acetic Acid, Glacial Meyer Catalog #0040

steps.title: We performed the colorimetric reaction in...
steps.instruction: We performed the colorimetric reaction in a 15 min boiling water bath incubation. The color formed is read at 460 nm using a spectrophotometer against a blank with water treated in the same manner. The absorbance at 460 nm is divided by 0.038 (the absorbance for 1 pg of citrulline, giving the amount of micrograms of citrulline contained). 00:15:00, boiling water bath (Beckman Coulter, DU 640, spectrophotometer)

steps.title: Based on the technique of Green, L
steps.instruction: Based on the technique of Green, L.C. et al. (1982). Analysis of Nitrtate, nitrite, and [15N]Nitrate in Biological Fluids. Analytical Biochemistry, 126, pp. 131-138, modified by Dr. Rolando Hernandez Munoz (IFC, UNAM, Mexico). Content of nitrites was determined in neutralized perchloric extracts of RBC and serum. A sample of 0.5 ml acid-extract was incubated 20 min at room temperature with the 0.5 ml of Griess reagent (0.1% naphtyl-ethylene diamine dihydrichloride and 1% sulfanilamide in 5% phosphoric acid). After developing the color, assays were read at 550 nm using a spectrophotometer, contrasting with a standard curve for sodium nitrate in deionized water. 00:20:00, incubation time N-(1-Naphthyl)ethylenediamine dihydrochloride Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #222488 Sulfanilamide Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #S9251 Phosphoric acid Meyer Catalog #7664-38-2 Sodium nitrate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #S5506 (Beckman Coulter, DU 640, spectrophotometer)
